claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 12 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit)
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority; Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance

Child is not in local authority care

Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

First, I need to ask: **Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me how many children you have and their ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have children I'm responsible for. I have two children - Alex who is 12 years old, and Blake who is 6 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex (12 years old) and Blake (6 years old).

Let me ask you a few questions to determine your eligibility:

**Do you currently live in the UK, or are you a member of HM Forces or a Crown servant posted overseas?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to ask about your relationship to the children:

**Are Alex and Blake living with you, or does someone else have main responsibility for their day-to-day care?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, it's a bit complicated. Blake lives with me, but Alex doesn't - Alex lives with their other parent.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. This is an important distinction because Child Benefit eligibility is assessed separately for each child.

Let me ask about Alex first: **Even though Alex lives with their other parent, do you contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit rate towards Alex's upkeep?** (This would currently be at least £25.60 per week for the eldest child or £16.95 per week for additional children.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I do contribute towards Alex's upkeep - I help with clothes and things like that - but it's not quite that much. I give about £20 a week.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex lives with their other parent and you contribute less than the Child Benefit rate, the other parent would typically be the one eligible to claim for Alex (as they have the main day-to-day care).

Now let me ask a few more questions about Blake, who lives with you:

**Is Blake currently in hospital, in care (such as local authority care), or in any type of residential educational establishment for more than 12 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in hospital or in care or anything like that. Blake lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are you or your partner currently receiving certain other benefits for Blake, such as Income Support, income-based Jobseeker's Allowance, income-related Employment and Support Allowance, or Pension Credit that includes an amount for Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not receiving any of those benefits for Blake.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

**Do you have immigration status in the UK that allows you to claim benefits, or are you subject to immigration control (such as having 'no recourse to public funds' on your visa)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have any immigration restrictions - I'm able to claim benefits.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I've completed my assessment of your Child Benefit eligibility. Here's my decision:

## **Eligibility Decision**

**For Alex (12 years old): NOT ELIGIBLE**
- Alex does not live with you - Alex lives with their other parent who has main day-to-day care responsibility
- While you do contribute towards Alex's upkeep (£20 per week), this is less than the Child Benefit rate (£25.60 per week for an eldest child or £16.95 for additional children)
- Under UK rules, when a child lives with someone else, you can only claim Child Benefit if you contribute at least as much as the Child Benefit rate towards their upkeep
- The parent Alex lives with would be the eligible claimant

**For Blake (6 years old): ELIGIBLE ✓**
- Blake is under 16 years old
- Blake lives with you and you have main responsibility for their day-to-day care
- You live in the UK
- Blake is not in hospital or care for more than 12 weeks
- You are not receiving other benefits that include an amount for Blake
- You have immigration status that allows you to claim benefits

## **Summary**
You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake. You can apply online through the government website or by post using the CH2 claim form. However, you cannot claim for Alex as Alex lives with the other parent and your contributions don't meet the required threshold.

reasonings: You are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex because: (1) Alex does not live with you - Alex lives with their other parent who has main day-to-day care responsibility, and (2) while you do contribute towards Alex's upkeep (£20 per week), this is less than the Child Benefit rate (£25.60 per week for an eldest child or £16.95 for additional children). Under UK rules, when a child lives with someone else, you can only claim Child Benefit if you contribute at least as much as the Child Benefit rate towards their upkeep. The parent Alex lives with would be the eligible claimant.

You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake because: (1) Blake is under 16 years old (currently 6 years old), (2) Blake lives with you and you have main responsibility for their day-to-day care, (3) you live in the UK, (4) Blake is not in hospital or care for more than 12 weeks, (5) you are not receiving other benefits that include an amount for Blake, and (6) you have immigration status that allows you to claim benefits. You meet all the eligibility criteria for claiming Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children but different circumstances apply to each. For Alex (12), who lives with the other parent, the claimant does not contribute enough to meet the threshold for claiming when a child lives elsewhere. For Blake (6), who lives with the claimant, all eligibility criteria are met. Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake only.

child_evaluations.reasoning: You are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex because: (1) Alex does not live with you - Alex lives with their other parent who has main day-to-day care responsibility, and (2) while you do contribute towards Alex's upkeep (£20 per week), this is less than the Child Benefit rate (£25.60 per week for an eldest child or £16.95 for additional children). Under UK rules, when a child lives with someone else, you can only claim Child Benefit if you contribute at least as much as the Child Benefit rate towards their upkeep. The parent Alex lives with would be the eligible claimant.

You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake because: (1) Blake is under 16 years old (currently 6 years old), (2) Blake lives with you and you have main responsibility for their day-to-day care, (3) you live in the UK, (4) Blake is not in hospital or care for more than 12 weeks, (5) you are not receiving other benefits that include an amount for Blake, and (6) you have immigration status that allows you to claim benefits. You meet all the eligibility criteria for claiming Child Benefit for Blake.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children but different circumstances apply to each. For Alex (12), who lives with the other parent, the claimant does not contribute enough to meet the threshold for claiming when a child lives elsewhere. For Blake (6), who lives with the claimant, all eligibility criteria are met. Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake only.
